page: Amazon Web Services r8in.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features dedicated Intel processors operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, delivering 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs via hyperthreading with 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage operations rely on external network-attached volumes. To support high-throughput storage and network traffic, the server provides a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal design eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing resource density for large-scale enterprise applications. The combination of high memory capacity and 300 Gbps network bandwidth makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and real-time data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r8in.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Powered by dedicated Intel processors running at 3.9 GHz, it provides 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs. The instance features 1536.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. To facilitate high-speed data transfers, the server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. This hardware configuration is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale database deployments, and high-throughput network operations.
